heading: Activities of savings associations
label: 1831e Activities of savings associations

children.text: the Corporation has determined that the activity would pose no significant risk to the Deposit Insurance Fund; and
the savings association is and continues to be in compliance with the fully phased-in capital standards prescribed under .section 1464(t) of this title

children.heading: Differences of magnitude between State and Federal powers
children.label: b Differences of magnitude between State and Federal powers

children.text: the Corporation has not determined that engaging in that amount of the activity poses any significant risk to the Deposit Insurance Fund; and
the savings association chartered under State law is and continues to be in compliance with the fully phased-in capital standards prescribed under .section 1464(t) of this title

children.heading: Equity investments by State savings associations
children.label: c Equity investments by State savings associations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subsections (a) and (b), a savings association chartered under State law may not directly acquire or retain any equity investment of a type or in an amount that is not permissible for a Federal savings association.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount that the association proposes to acquire or retain; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the activities in which the service corporation engages; and

children.label: B
children.text: the savings association is and continues to be in compliance with the fully phased-in capital standards prescribed under .section 1464(t) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation shall require any savings association to divest any equity investment the retention of which is not permissible under paragraph (1) or (2) as quickly as can be prudently done, and in any event not later than .July 1, 1994

children.heading: Treatment of noncompliance during divestment
children.label: B Treatment of noncompliance during divestment
children.text: With respect to any equity investment held by any savings association on , the savings association shall be deemed not to be in violation of the prohibition in paragraph (1) or (2) on retaining such investment so long as the savings association complies with any applicable requirement established by the Corporation pursuant to subparagraph (A) for divesting such investments.May 1, 1989

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No savings association may, directly or through a subsidiary, acquire or retain any corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness as established by the Corporation.

children.heading: Exception for securities held by qualified affiliate
children.label: 2 Exception for securities held by qualified affiliate
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply with respect to any corporate debt security which is acquired and retained by any qualified affiliate of a savings association.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a stock savings association, an affiliate other than a subsidiary or an insured depository institution; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a mutual savings association, a subsidiary other than an insured depository institution, so long as all of the savings association’s investments in and extensions of credit to the subsidiary are deducted from the savings association’s capital.

children.heading: Certain securities not included
children.label: B Certain securities not included
children.text: The term “corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness as established by the Corporation” does not include any obligation issued or guaranteed by a corporation that may be held by a Federal savings association without limitation as to percentage of assets under subparagraph (D), (E), or (F) of .section 1464(c)(1) of this title

children.heading: Transfer of corporate debt security in exchange for a qualified note
children.label: e Transfer of corporate debt security in exchange for a qualified note
A
children.text: any holding company which controls 80 percent or more of the shares of such insured savings association; or

children.label: B
children.text: any company other than an insured savings association, or any subsidiary of any insured savings association, 80 percent or more of the shares of which are controlled by such holding company,

children.heading: Conditions for exchange of security for qualified note
children.label: 2 Conditions for exchange of security for qualified note
i
children.text: remains in compliance with applicable capital requirements; or

children.label: ii
children.text: adopts and complies with a capital plan acceptable to the Comptroller of the Currency or the Corporation, as appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: the company to which the corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness established by the Corporation is transferred is not a bank holding company, an insured savings association, or a direct or indirect subsidiary of such holding company or insured savings association;

children.label: C
children.text: before the end of the 90-day period beginning on , the insured savings association notifies the Comptroller of the Currency or the Corporation, as appropriate, of such association’s intention to transfer the corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness established by the Corporation to the savings and loan holding company or the subsidiary of such holding company;August 9, 1989

children.label: i
children.text: before the end of the 1-year period beginning on , in the case of an insured savings association that, as of , is controlled by a savings and loan holding company; orAugust 9, 1989August 9, 1989

children.label: ii
children.text: before the end of the 2-year period beginning on , in the case of a savings association that is not, as of , a subsidiary of a savings and loan holding company;August 9, 1989August 9, 1989

children.label: E
children.text: the insured savings association receives in exchange for the corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness established by the Corporation the fair market value of such security;

children.label: i
children.text: approved the transaction; and

children.label: ii
children.text: determined that the transfer represents a complete and effective divestiture of the corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness established by the Corporation and is in compliance with the provisions of this subsection; and

children.label: G
children.text: any gain on the sale of the corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness established by the Corporation is recognized, and included for applicable regulatory capital requirements, by the insured savings association only at such time and to the extent that the insured savings association receives payment of principal on the note in cash in excess of the fair market value of the transferred corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness established by the Corporation as carried on the accounts of the insured savings association immediately prior to the transfer.

children.label: A
children.text: is at all times fully secured by the corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness established by the Corporation transferred in exchange for the note, or by other collateral of at least equivalent value that is acceptable to the Comptroller of the Currency or the Corporation, as appropriate;

children.label: i
children.text: prevent any action to encumber or impair the value of the collateral referred to in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: allow the sale of the corporate debt security that does not meet standards of credit-worthiness established by the Corporation if the proceeds of the sale are reinvested in assets of equivalent value;

children.label: C
children.text: is on market terms, including interest rate, which must in all cases be above the insured savings association’s borrowing rate for similar term funds;

children.label: D
children.text: is fully repayable over a period of time not to exceed 5 years from the date of transfer;

children.label: E
children.text: is repaid with annual principal payments at least as large as would be necessary to repay the note within 5 years if it were on a level payment amortization schedule and the interest rate for the first year of repayment were fixed throughout the amortization period;

children.label: F
children.text: is fully guaranteed by each holding company of the insured savings association that acquires such note; and

children.label: G
children.text: is repaid in full in cash in accordance with its terms and this subsection.

children.heading: Failure to repay on schedule
children.label: 4 Failure to repay on schedule
children.text: The exemption provided by this subsection from subsections (a), (b), and (c) of and any other applicable provision of Federal or State law shall terminate immediately if the insured savings association or any affiliate of such association fails to comply with the terms of the qualified note or this subsection.section 1468 of this title

children.heading: Determinations
children.label: f Determinations
children.text: The Corporation shall make determinations under this section by regulation or order.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “activity” includes acquiring or retaining any investment.

children.heading: Divestiture of certain assets
children.label: 2 Divestiture of certain assets
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), subsections (a) and (b) shall not be construed to require a savings association to divest itself of any assets acquired before .August 9, 1989
any other authority of the Corporation; or
any authority of the Comptroller of the Currency, of the Corporation, or of a State to impose more stringent restrictions.
